# bgviewer

> bgviewer is a free, open-source (MIT) menu-bar kill-switch for macOS background
> services. Activity Monitor shows you the process; bgviewer shows why it keeps
> coming back — and stops it at the launchd level, where resurrection is decided.
> Universal binary (Apple Silicon + Intel), macOS 13+, ~700 KB, zero dependencies,
> zero telemetry.

What it does:

- Sees six sources in one dropdown: user launch agents (~/Library/LaunchAgents),
  machine-wide vendor agents (/Library/LaunchAgents), Homebrew services, every
  process holding a TCP port, resource hogs (≥15% CPU or ≥1 GB RAM with no port),
  and cron jobs (read-only).
- Stop uses `launchctl bootout`, which defeats KeepAlive so services stay stopped;
  stubborn processes get SIGTERM then SIGKILL, and every action is verified.
- Disable parks the agent's plist (never deletes it) and blocks auto-start until
  re-enabled with one click. Apple system processes are locked and untouchable.
- Opt-in alerts the moment a new dev server starts listening, even when closed.
- Storage tools: biggest forgotten files, a drill-down disk-map pie chart of any
  folder, and Dev Junk — regenerable build folders (node_modules, Python venvs,
  Rust target, CocoaPods, DerivedData) and app caches, deletable behind guard
  checks and a two-step confirm. Personal files are never deletable.
- Self-updates from GitHub Releases, verified against SHA-256 checksums and code
  signature, with automatic rollback. The only network calls are the release
  check (≤ every 6 h) and updates.

Install:

- One-liner: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AroraShreshth/bgviewer/main/install.sh | bash`
- Homebrew: `brew tap arorashreshth/bgviewer https://github.com/AroraShreshth/bgviewer && brew install --cask arorashreshth/bgviewer/bgviewer --no-quarantine`
- Manual: download the zip from the releases page, drag to /Applications, then
  `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine /Applications/bgviewer.app` (not notarized yet).

Links:

- [Website](https://bgviewer.shreshtharora.com)
- [Source code](https://github.com/AroraShreshth/bgviewer): full Swift source, MIT
- [Releases](https://github.com/AroraShreshth/bgviewer/releases): signed zips + SHA-256 checksums
- [Issues](https://github.com/AroraShreshth/bgviewer/issues)

Author: Shreshth Arora (https://github.com/AroraShreshth)
